name: Greek entity themis
shortDescription: Themis is the ancient Greek Titaness of divine law, order, fairness, custom, and morality. She is a personification of natural law and societal order.
description: Themis, in Greek mythology, is a Titaness, daughter of Uranus (Heaven) and Gaia (Earth). She is the embodiment of divine law, order, fairness, custom, and morality. More than just law in the human sense, Themis represents the natural and eternal principles that govern the universe and human society. She is often depicted as a wise counselor and advisor to Zeus, and she is associated with prophecy and oracles, notably presiding over the Oracle at Delphi before it was taken over by Apollo. Themis's role extends beyond dispensing justice; she is concerned with maintaining harmony and balance in all aspects of existence. Her influence is seen in the proper conduct of religious ceremonies, the hospitality shown to strangers, and the respect for established customs. She is also considered to be the mother of the Horae (Seasons) and the Moirai (Fates) with Zeus, further emphasizing her connection to the cyclical nature of time and destiny. Her presence underscores the importance of adhering to divine and natural laws for a harmonious existence. She is a primordial figure, representing the foundational principles upon which civilization is built.
